method: For each candidate finding, three sources were searched before write-up: (1) the Docling GitHub issue tracker via `gh search issues` / `gh issue list` on docling-project/docling (and the underlying engine repos where relevant), (2) the official docs + HF model card pages, (3) general web / SERP top ~20. Each candidate is classified KNOWN-ISSUE (link), DOCUMENTED, or NO-PUBLIC-HIT. This file persists the query strings and outcomes so the 'zero-hit' claims are re-checkable; issue existence/open-closed state and SERP snapshots are re-verified at fresh-clone time (see RM Gaps / audit deferred list #7).
note_on_reproverification: Issue numbers and their open/closed state are volatile (938 open issues, weekly releases). The links below are the load-bearing prior-record pointers; a fresh audit re-confirms each via `gh api repos/docling-project/docling/issues/<n>`.

candidates.candidate: Heavy torch/opencv/transformers deps pulled even for HTML-only use (~1.3 GB venv)
queries: gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling "lightweight install" torch
gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling slim OR minimal dependencies
web: docling install size torch gpu cpu-only 7GB
candidates.our_addition: measured on-disk per-dependency breakdown (not just the complaint)

queries: gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling __version__
gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling "has no attribute" version

candidates.candidate: HTML runs a BeautifulSoup backend, not the layout/TableFormer models
queries: docs: docling architecture concepts format backend pipeline
gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling HTML TableFormer backend
candidates.verdict: DOCUMENTED (architecture), not stated plainly in one place
candidates.our_addition: confirmed by measurement (no ML model on HTML path)

queries: gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling nav footer boilerplate
gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling main content extraction readability
candidates.our_addition: residue percentage on real pages (13.3% Wikipedia)

queries: gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling borderless table
gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling whitespace delimited columns table
candidates.our_addition: qualifies it: a borderless table with header rule + aligned columns recovered perfectly (T2)

queries: gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling merged rows columns table
gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling rowspan colspan
candidates.our_addition: qualifies it: simple colspan/rowspan flattened correctly here (T3/T4b); complex/irregular remain open

candidates.candidate: Isolated small table on a sparse page classified as Picture and dropped (page-sparsity trigger)
queries: gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling table detected as picture
gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling table dropped image classification
gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling sparse page single table
web: docling table classified as picture dropped no error
candidates.verdict: NO-PUBLIC-HIT for the page-sparsity trigger (adjacent issue exists)
prior_record: #3495 (table detected as Table AND Picture) — closest, but does not state the same-table-drops-when-alone / converts-when-embedded trigger
candidates.our_addition: the controlled A/B isolating page context (pdf_sparse_page_ab.json): same table, tables=0/picture when alone vs tables=1 when surrounded by text

candidates.candidate: First-run PDF model download SIZE in MB
queries: docs: docling model_catalog size MB GB
gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling model download size MB
web: docling models download size how big TableFormer layout
prior_record: official model catalog names models but publishes no MB/GB
candidates.our_addition: measured on-disk model footprint (~506 MiB HF cache + RapidOCR weights); accuracy (TEDS) IS published separately

candidates.candidate: Default OCR engine is now RapidOCR (not EasyOCR)
queries: gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling default OCR RapidOCR EasyOCR
pyproject.toml feat-ocr-rapidocr core extra
web: docling default OCR engine EasyOCR
prior_record: current pyproject bundles feat-ocr-rapidocr; EasyOCR is an extra (#3227). Most existing blogs/FAQ still say EasyOCR default — stale.
candidates.our_addition: confirmed by watching the PP-OCRv4 download at first run

queries: gh search issues --repo docling-project/docling import slow cold start seconds
web: transformers import slow 15-45s cold
prior_record: transformers import cost is a known transformers property (huggingface/transformers#16863) — not a Docling-specific published fact
candidates.our_addition: Docling-specific cold-import wall-clock (~30 s); do not imply the transformers slowness itself is undiscovered
